2605.29357.title: PassNet: Scaling Large Language Models for Graph Compiler Pass Generation
2605.29357.paper_summary: Modern tensor compilers such as TorchInductor deliver substantial speedups on mainstream models, yet face a systematic performance ceiling on long-tail workloads -- our profiling shows that 43% of real-world subgraphs experience end-to-end slowdowns under default compilation. While LLMs offer a path toward automated optimization, existing efforts focus on standalone kernel generation. We argue that pass generation -- where LLMs author structured graph transformations that integrate directly into compiler pipelines -- is the more appropriate abstraction. We propose PassNet, the first large-scale ecosystem for LLM-based compiler pass generation, comprising: (1) PassNet-Dataset, over 18K unique computational graphs from 100K real-world models; and (2) PassBench, 200 curated long-tail fusible tasks (comprising 2,060 subgraphs in total) evaluated under the Error-aware Speedup Score (ES_t) -- a metric unifying correctness, stability, and performance -- with layered integrity defenses against systematic LLM exploitation. Experiments reveal that PassBench is both highly discriminative and genuinely unsaturated: the best frontier model trails TorchInductor by 37% in aggregate, yet on individual subgraphs LLMs achieve up to 3x speedup over the same compiler -- indicating that the bottleneck is consistency, not capability. Fine-tuning a small model on merely ~4K PassNet trajectories yields a 2.67x improvement approaching frontier-model performance, demonstrating substantial headroom and validating PassNet as live training infrastructure for advancing LLM-driven compiler optimization. All data, benchmarks, and tooling are publicly available.
2605.29357.paper_summary_zh: 現代的張量編譯器，如 TorchInductor，在主流模型上提供了顯著的加速，但在長尾工作負載上面臨系統性的性能瓶頸——我們的分析顯示，43% 的真實子圖在默認編譯下經歷了端到端的減速。雖然 LLM 提供了一條自動優化的途徑，但現有的努力集中在獨立的內核生成上。我們認為，通過生成——即 LLM 編寫結構化圖變換，這些變換直接整合到編譯器管道中——是更合適的抽象。我們提出了 PassNet，首個基於 LLM 的編譯器通過生成的大規模生態系統，包括：(1) PassNet-Dataset，來自 100K 真實模型的超過 18K 獨特計算圖；以及 (2) PassBench，200 個策劃的長尾可融合任務（共包括 2,060 個子圖），在錯誤感知加速分數（ES_t）下進行評估——這是一個統一正確性、穩定性和性能的指標——並針對系統性 LLM 利用設計了分層的完整性防禦。實驗顯示，PassBench 既具有高度的區分性，又真正未飽和：最佳前沿模型在總體上落後於 TorchInductor 37%，但在個別子圖上，LLM 在同一編譯器上實現了高達 3 倍的加速——這表明瓶頸在於一致性，而非能力。僅在約 4K PassNet 軌跡上微調一個小模型即可實現 2.67 倍的改進，接近前沿模型的性能，顯示出顯著的提升空間，並驗證了 PassNet 作為推進 LLM 驅動編譯器優化的實時訓練基礎設施。所有數據、基準和工具均可公開獲得。
2605.29357.author: Yiqun Liu et.al.
2605.29357.authors: Yiqun Liu, Yingsheng Wu, Ruqi Yang, Enrong Zheng, Honglei Qiu, Sijun He, Tai Liang, Jingjing Wu, Yuhan Zhou, Yiwei Zhang, Dongyan Chen, Weihan Yi, Xinqi Li, Siqi Bao
